channel.title: Comments for page "Hartliss Detective Agency"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Hartliss Detective Agency"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Delightful. Who knew that what 20s gangster stories were missing was excessive gore?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…Holy crap I never realized how good Foundationverse noir would be until now. This gives me a good Constantine sort of vibe, but with 20's American noir aesthetic instead of 80's British punk, and… and I love it. Please write more.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh man, that picture is noir as fuck.

Also, I just realized I really should credit you in the author post above — since a lot of the stuff I'm using for this storyline is just straight-up stolen from several of your pieces.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Based sheerly on To Be Noir Not To Be, I remain unconvinced that you couldn't execute it with at least 20% more panache (maybe even 25%!).

Besides, you already cracked open the floodgate with that piece; all I'm doing is swinging a Sarkic-shaped hammer at it to try and blow it open a little wider (in hopes of drowning us all in a sea of hard-boiled nonsense).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm salty as shit you beat me to the noir thing, but hell if you didn't execute it with way more panache than I think I could've ever mustered. Upvoted in eager anticipation of the sequel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm really pleased with this. +1 Eager to see more :) Weird fiction noir is the best.

Edit: An image that I feel goes well with Sarkic noir: http://i.imgur.com/wtLHRR7.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A thick, meaty tale rich with noir metaphors that make the flow of exposition smooth like a fine wine down the throat. I'm a sucker for gangster stories and I hope to see more Sarkic works in this vein.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The story flows a lot better than the last draft I saw, this is highly enjoyable. Hearty and enthusiastic upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 20's era urban fantasy? And noir to boot? Sign me the fuck up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for a good kissy jump boy, just a rowdy meaty boy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty much, yeah — I'm planning on getting more indepth about how this works in the next part, but the basic idea was taken straight from Metaphysician's SCP-2480. He uses magic to accomplish what they use DMT for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, can this guy naturally enter the world beneath the surface world like Sitra Achra personnel do via drugs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted at the use of the word "moxie."

Also, the ending is a great addition, making for a very gripping conclusion. I hope that we get to see more of our narrator and how he became so knowledgable and unfazed by mind-bending weirdness.

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Credit
– hide block

If I'm forgetting anyone, please poke me and say so!

  • Roget
  • Gaffney
  • DrChandra

And Metaphysician's pieces — specifically Sarkicism Hub, SCP-2133, and SCP-2480 — all of which I'm shamelessly stealing from for this series.

So, this is my shot at a multi-part series; there's going to be three parts in total (hopefully, dependent on response). It will (again, hopefully) involve the Chicago Spirit, Sarkicists, and a few other things. It's also super-gorey, so I apologize in advance for that.

(The goal here is to also introduce a few concepts that I've been really wanting to explore — particularly my take on neo-Sarkicism, and my own idea, the Gallant Society)

If anyone's curious, this tale takes place in 1926 Chicago — at the height of the conflict between Al Capone's Outfit and Hymie Weiss' North Side Gang.
